title: OOP Reference Exams
description: Closed exams reviewed from the converted OOP reference Markdown files.

exams.title: Reference - C++ for Everyone
exams.description: Questions from c-for-every-one.md, focusing on classes, pointers, inheritance, and polymorphism.

questions.prompt: Which route does C++ for Everyone take before class design?
choices: It first builds control structures, procedural decomposition, arrays, and algorithms
It begins directly with virtual destructors
It teaches only graphical user interfaces
questions.explanation: The reference introduces core procedural programming and arrays before moving to classes.

questions.prompt: Which chapters introduce the object-oriented features in C++ for Everyone?
questions.explanation: The table of contents places classes before inheritance and virtual-function polymorphism.

questions.prompt: Why are pointers important before polymorphism in this reference?
choices: Base-class pointers can refer to objects from different derived classes
questions.explanation: The reference connects pointers with later polymorphism through base and derived class objects.

questions.prompt: What should class data fields usually be according to the class-design guidance?
questions.explanation: The reference emphasizes encapsulation through private data manipulated by member functions.

questions.prompt: What is the role of a constructor in the class chapter?
choices: Initialize an object into a valid state when it is created
Return a Boolean value from every class
questions.explanation: Constructors avoid random/uninitialized member values by setting state at object creation.

questions.prompt: What does 'Discovering Classes' ask the programmer to identify?
choices: Objects, responsibilities, state, and behavior in the problem domain
questions.explanation: The problem-solving section focuses on finding useful classes from the domain model.

questions.prompt: What relationship does inheritance express in this reference?
choices: An is-a relationship between a general base class and a specialized derived class
questions.explanation: The glossary and inheritance chapter describe inheritance as an is-a relation.

questions.prompt: Why is private inheritance described as rarely useful?
choices: It hides inherited public behavior and works against ordinary is-a inheritance
It makes every base member public
It is required for all derived classes
questions.explanation: The reference warns that private inheritance usually violates the spirit of inheritance.

questions.prompt: What enables variation in behavior at run time?
questions.explanation: The inheritance chapter uses virtual functions to vary behavior at runtime.

questions.prompt: Why can an array of base-class pointers be useful?
choices: It can store references to objects from different derived classes through a common base type
It deletes every derived object automatically
questions.explanation: The reference shows that base pointers allow mixed derived objects in one collection.

questions.prompt: What is the advantage of using virtual functions instead of type tags?
choices: New derived behavior can be added with less change to existing client code
Every class must expose its data
questions.explanation: The reference says virtual functions make programs easier to extend without changing the calling code.

questions.prompt: How does the reference define polymorphism in the glossary area?
choices: Selecting among functions with the same name according to the object being used
Storing only one value in memory
Forcing every class to be final
Replacing every pointer with a macro
questions.explanation: The reference connects polymorphism to choosing the appropriate function implementation.

questions.prompt: What does a dangling pointer refer to?
choices: A pointer that no longer points to valid memory
A pointer to a public member only
A pointer that stores a class name
questions.explanation: The pointer chapter lists dangling pointers as a common pointer mistake.

questions.prompt: Why are references useful in function parameters?
choices: They let a function operate on the original object without copying it
They turn a class into a string
questions.explanation: The pointer/reference chapter covers passing arguments by reference for direct access to the original value.

questions.prompt: Which statement best fits the reference's class-design style?
choices: Expose a clean public interface while hiding implementation details
Put all data in global variables
Use inheritance for every possible relationship
questions.explanation: The reference's OOP chapters consistently favor encapsulation and public member functions over exposed data.

exams.title: Reference - C++ in One Hour a Day
exams.description: Questions from c-in-one-hour-a-day.md, focusing on modern C++ OOP lessons.

questions.prompt: Which part of a basic C++ program contains the starting point of execution?
questions.explanation: The anatomy lesson identifies `main()` as the body where program execution begins.

questions.prompt: What does `#include` do in a C++ program?
choices: It asks the preprocessor to include declarations from a header
questions.explanation: The reference covers `#include` as a preprocessor directive used before compilation.

questions.prompt: What is a function prototype used for?
choices: Declaring a function's interface before its full definition is seen
questions.explanation: The functions lesson distinguishes prototypes, definitions, calls, and arguments.

questions.prompt: Why are references useful when passing arguments to functions?
choices: They allow the function to work with the caller's original value
They always copy the whole array
questions.explanation: The reference teaches passing arguments by reference as a way to operate on original data.

questions.prompt: What does dereferencing a pointer mean?
choices: Accessing the data at the address stored in the pointer
questions.explanation: The pointer lesson introduces `*` as the dereference operator for pointed data.

questions.prompt: What creates dynamic memory in the pointer lesson?
questions.explanation: The reference pairs `new` and `delete` for dynamic allocation and release.

questions.prompt: In the classes lesson, what is an object?
questions.explanation: The lesson identifies objects as instances created from class definitions.

questions.prompt: Which operator accesses members through an object pointer?
questions.explanation: The classes lesson contrasts dot access on objects with pointer member access through `->`.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of `private` in a class?
choices: Hide implementation details and restrict direct access
questions.explanation: The reference discusses data abstraction via private members.

questions.prompt: What can a friend function or friend class do?
choices: Access private members when explicitly trusted by the class
questions.explanation: The friend section shows external functions/classes being granted special access.

questions.prompt: What does inheritance implement in the C++ in One Hour a Day lessons?
choices: A relationship where derived classes reuse and extend base-class behavior
questions.explanation: The inheritance lesson follows classes and objects and focuses on derived classes.

questions.prompt: What lesson follows inheritance and focuses on many forms?
questions.explanation: The table of contents places polymorphism after implementing inheritance.

questions.prompt: What is operator overloading used for?
choices: Defining meaningful operator behavior for user-defined types
questions.explanation: The operator lesson covers operator types and operator overloading for classes.

questions.prompt: Why does the reference prefer templates over macros when possible?
questions.explanation: The macros/templates lesson notes that templates are reusable and type-safe.

questions.prompt: What is the Standard Template Library mainly introduced as?
choices: A library of reusable template-based containers and algorithms
questions.explanation: The STL lesson introduces reusable standard library components built around templates.

exams.title: Reference - Object-Oriented Programming in C++
exams.description: Questions from objectorientedprogramming-c.md by Robert Lafore.

questions.prompt: Why does the Lafore reference say object-oriented programming is needed?
choices: Large procedural programs become difficult to manage and modify
questions.explanation: The early OOP chapter contrasts procedural growth with the object-oriented approach.

questions.prompt: Which group is presented as central to object-oriented languages?
choices: Objects, classes, inheritance, reusability, and polymorphism
questions.explanation: The reference's big-picture chapter lists the core OOP characteristics.

questions.prompt: What does a class provide in the Lafore reference?
choices: A user-defined type that combines data and functions
questions.explanation: The objects/classes chapter treats classes as the central way to form objects.

questions.prompt: Why is reusability a payoff of inheritance?
choices: A derived class can extend an existing class instead of rewriting it
questions.explanation: The inheritance chapter says derived classes reuse tested base-class capabilities.

questions.prompt: What is operator overloading intended to improve?
choices: Natural syntax for operations on user-defined objects
The number of required header files only
questions.explanation: The operator-overloading chapter shows object operations written with familiar operators.

questions.prompt: Why should operator overloading be used carefully?
choices: Misleading operator meanings can make code harder to understand
questions.explanation: The chapter includes pitfalls of operator overloading and conversion.

questions.prompt: What does public inheritance normally model?
choices: A derived object is a specialized form of the base object
Two unrelated files share a name
A function has no return type
questions.explanation: The inheritance chapter uses public inheritance for ordinary specialization.

questions.prompt: What can create ambiguity in multiple inheritance?
choices: The same base-class member may be inherited through more than one path
questions.explanation: The multiple-inheritance chapter discusses ambiguous member lookup.

questions.prompt: What are virtual functions used for in Lafore's reference?
choices: Calling the correct derived-class function through a base pointer or reference
questions.explanation: The virtual-functions chapter explains dynamic dispatch through base-class access.

questions.prompt: What is an abstract class in the virtual-functions chapter?
choices: A class intended as a base that cannot be instantiated directly when it has pure virtual functions
A class with only public data
A class with no functions at all
A class that replaces every derived class
questions.explanation: The reference covers abstract classes and pure virtual functions after virtual dispatch.

questions.prompt: Why are friend functions described as controversial?
choices: They cross class boundaries by accessing private data
They are required for every class
questions.explanation: The virtual-functions/friend section says friend functions should be used sparingly because they affect data integrity boundaries.

questions.prompt: When are friend functions especially convenient?
choices: When an operation must work symmetrically with objects from different classes or operands
When no class data is needed
When every member is already public
When there is no operator involved
questions.explanation: The reference connects friend functions with bridging classes and some operator-overloading cases.

questions.prompt: What problem do function templates solve?
choices: Avoiding duplicate functions that differ mainly by data type
questions.explanation: The templates chapter starts with function templates before class templates.

questions.prompt: What do class templates allow?
choices: Generic classes whose stored type can vary by instantiation
questions.explanation: The chapter moves from function templates to class templates for reusable generic classes.

questions.prompt: What is the STL in the Lafore reference?
choices: The Standard Template Library, a collection of template-based containers and algorithms
questions.explanation: The reference dedicates a chapter to the Standard Template Library after templates and exceptions.

exams.title: Reference - Arabic OOP Notes
exams.description: Questions from البرمجة-الشيئية-أ-حذيفة.md, using the readable OOP/C++ concepts from the converted Markdown.

questions.prompt: Which feature means showing essential behavior while hiding unnecessary detail?
questions.explanation: The Arabic OOP reference lists abstraction as a feature of OOP.

questions.prompt: Which feature combines data and functions inside one class/object unit?
questions.explanation: The reference lists encapsulation as grouping data and behavior together.

questions.prompt: What does data hiding protect?
choices: The internal data of an object from uncontrolled direct access
questions.explanation: The reference treats data hiding as a key benefit of private/protected/public access control.

questions.prompt: Which access level allows access mainly from inside the class?
questions.explanation: The access-level section distinguishes private, protected, and public visibility.

questions.prompt: Which access level supports access from derived classes?
questions.explanation: Protected members are presented as accessible in inheritance contexts.

questions.prompt: Which C++ operator defines a member function outside its class body?
questions.explanation: The reference table of contents includes the scope resolution operator `::`.

questions.prompt: What is a constructor responsible for?
choices: Preparing object state when an object is created
Returning the object as an integer
questions.explanation: The reference has constructor and destructor sections as core class-function topics.

questions.prompt: What is a destructor responsible for?
choices: Cleanup when an object is destroyed
questions.explanation: Constructors and destructors are paired in the reference's class-function sections.

questions.prompt: Which operators are used for dynamic memory allocation and release?
questions.explanation: The Arabic reference table of contents explicitly includes `new` and `delete`.

questions.prompt: What does `this` refer to in a member function?
choices: The current object executing the member function
The parent folder of the source file
The next object in an array
questions.explanation: The reference includes a section on the `this` pointer/object reference.

questions.prompt: What can a friend function do?
choices: Access class data when the class grants it friend access
Disable encapsulation for all classes globally
questions.explanation: The reference includes friend functions and friend classes as special access mechanisms.

questions.prompt: What is function overloading?
choices: Using the same function name with different parameter lists
questions.explanation: The reference distinguishes function overloading from operator overloading.

questions.prompt: What is operator overloading?
choices: Giving an operator a class-specific meaning
questions.explanation: The reference contains a major section on operators overloading.

questions.prompt: What does multiple inheritance allow?
choices: A class to inherit from more than one base class
A function to return multiple unrelated values automatically
A variable to have no type
questions.explanation: The reference's inheritance section includes multiple inheritance.

questions.prompt: What is a virtual base class used for?
choices: Avoiding duplicate base-class copies in certain multiple-inheritance hierarchies
Forcing every data member to be public
questions.explanation: The table of contents ends with virtual base class after multiple inheritance.

questions.prompt: What should the Hard Currency Converter validate?
choices: That money values entered by the user are numeric
That the user enters only letters
That no exchange amount is entered
questions.explanation: The project requires validation so conversion works only with numeric input.

questions.prompt: What should happen for invalid currency-converter input?
questions.explanation: The reference says invalid data should produce an error message.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of the ATM Card project?
choices: Check whether card data is valid before simple financial operations
questions.explanation: The ATM project centers on card validation before operations such as withdrawal, transfer, and balance viewing.

questions.prompt: Which calculator modes are required by the Calculator project?
questions.explanation: The project asks for two calculator types: simple and scientific.

questions.prompt: What should the Calculator project check before operating?
choices: Input validity and numeric data suitability
Whether a phone number is deleted
questions.explanation: The description says calculations should not happen unless entered values are numeric and suitable.

questions.prompt: Which student data can appear in the Student Management System?
choices: University number, name, address, major, level, subjects, grades, and GPA
questions.explanation: The reference lists these fields as examples for student management.

questions.prompt: How should the Student Management System treat stored data?
choices: Modify data during runtime without permanent file storage
Save everything permanently in a file
questions.explanation: The project says data is not saved permanently in a file; it is edited and deleted during execution.

questions.prompt: Which information is requested when adding a book in the Bookshop Management System?
choices: Book name, author name, and number of copies
questions.explanation: The bookshop project lists title/name, author, and copy count as book information.

questions.prompt: What should happen before deleting a book record?
questions.explanation: The bookshop project requires confirmation before deleting records.

questions.prompt: Which operations belong to the Bank Account Management System?
choices: Deposit, withdrawal, display, search, edit, and delete
questions.explanation: The bank account project lists account creation plus deposit, withdrawal, display, search, edit, and delete features.

questions.prompt: What should the Super Market Billing System show?
choices: At least seven products with prices and purchased-item totals
questions.explanation: The supermarket project asks to display several products, allow purchases, show item values, and calculate a total.

questions.prompt: Which features belong to the Phone Directory project?
choices: Add, display, edit, search, and delete phone records
questions.explanation: The phone directory project lists those record-management operations.

questions.prompt: Which data is suggested for the maintenance-department project?
choices: Department, device type, maintenance date, and condition before/after maintenance
questions.explanation: The maintenance project mentions reports for devices such as computers/printers and includes maintenance details.

questions.prompt: Which details are suggested for bus reservation?
choices: Bus number, bus brand, seat number, booked/free seats, and ticket price
questions.explanation: The bus reservation project lists those ticket and bus details.

questions.prompt: What general note is given for the projects?
choices: Simulate the system screens as much as possible while leaving room for project-specific movement
questions.explanation: The project reference closes by asking students to simulate system screens as much as possible.
